steps.title: Read the y-intercept from the graph
steps.content: The y-intercept is the point where the curve crosses the y-axis, which occurs at $x = 0$. From the graph, the curve passes through the point $(0, 9)$, so the y-intercept is $9$.

steps.title: Interpret x = 0 and the units of y in context
steps.content:
Since $x$ represents the number of years since the end of 1992, the value $x = 0$ corresponds to the end of 1992.

The variable $y$ is given in thousands of catalogs. Therefore, the y-intercept value of $9$ means 9 thousand catalogs, which equals $9 \times 1000 = 9,000$ catalogs.

The y-intercept represents the estimated number of catalogs sent at the end of 1992, which was 9,000.

That matches the correct answer — D is correct.
